name: learning-utils
documentation:
Python package to reduce iteration time for developing learning systems.

Use this command to install:

```
pip install learning_utils
```

modules.name: learning_utils.Memories
documentation: Replay buffer that can handle any number of things we need to remember for the network

methods.name: learning_utils.Memories.ReplayBuffer.add
documentation: Takes a variable number of arguments to store in a the replay buffer

methods.name: learning_utils.Memories.ReplayBuffer.sample
documentation:
Sample a batch of batch_size. 
Returns a zip object, so you will have to unpack the sample yourself.  

args:
    batch_size (int): Size of sample

returns: 
    batch (zip object): Your samples

methods.name: learning_utils.Memories.ReplayBuffer.len
documentation: Current size of the replay buffer

modules.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers
documentation: A way to track data in the training loop like rewards, losses, and other metrics

classes.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker

methods.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker.load_metrics
documentation:
Alternative constructor to load your metrics from a CSV file to continue training

args:
    path (str): where to load your metrics from i.e. path/to/file.csv

methods.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker.update
documentation:
Update your metrics. Be sure to input your metrics in the same order that you defined them in.
If you don't remember and don't want to look back in your code, use print_info() to see what order your metrics are in.

args:
    metrics (any): Metrics that you want to track. Be sure to put them in the right order. Use print_info() to see the order to input them in.

methods.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker.print_info
documentation: Prints the number of metrics you are tracking as well as the order to put in the update() function

methods.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker.get_metrics_df
documentation: Returns your metrics in a pandas dataframe

methods.name: learning_utils.DataTrackers.TrainingLoopTracker.save
documentation:
Saves your metrics to a csv file in path.

args:
    path (str): where to save your metrics i.e. path/to/file.csv
    index (bool): Whether to save with an index column or not

Easy way to track all of your metrics in the training loop like rewards, losses, etc.
Enter the names of the metrics you want to track as strings.

args:
    *metrics (str): Strings that contain the names of your metrics

attr:
    names (list of strings): 
    num_metrics (int): Number of metrics you are tracking
    metrics (list of lists): Metrics you are tracking
